steps.title: Find the clause boundary
steps.content: The sentence has two independent clauses: "The sand at Komodo Island Beach in Indonesia is a more unusual shade" and "deposits of crushed coral and other organic matter lend the sand a unique pink hue." The word "though" works as a parenthetical transition after the first clause.

steps.title: Punctuate the transition
steps.content: Choice B and choice D create comma splices because they use only commas between independent clauses. Choice C places the semicolon before "though," making the second part read awkwardly. The remaining choice sets off "though" with a comma and uses a semicolon before the next independent clause. The answer is A.
